page.content: In the Content Spending Overview by Format and Year, the table reveals that in 2020, television spending was £1.2B, which increased to £1.3B in 2021 and £1.5B in 2022, yielding a growth rate of 15%. Streaming expenditures rose from £800M in 2020 to £900M in 2021 and reached £1.1B in 2022, representing a 22% growth. Digital media, encompassing web content, started at £500M in 2020, grew to £600M in 2021, and was £700M in 2022, achieving a 17% growth rate.

lines.text: In the Content Spending Overview by Format and Year, the table reveals that in 2020, television spending was £1.2B, which
increased to £1.3B in 2021 and £1.5B in 2022, yielding a growth rate of 15%. Streaming expenditures rose from £800M in 2020
to £900M in 2021 and reached £1.1B in 2022, representing a 22% growth. Digital media, encompassing web content, started at
£500M in 2020, grew to £600M in 2021, and was £700M in 2022, achieving a 17% growth rate.

page.content: Total spending across all formats was £2.5B in 2020, £2.8B in 2021, and £3.3B in 2022, with an 18% growth. Advertising saw a rise from £300M in 2020 to £350M in 2021 and £400M in 2022, reflecting a 14% growth rate. Merchandising increased from £250M in 2020 to £300M in 2021 and reached £320M in 2022, growing at 7%. Live events spending went from £200M in 2020 to £250M in 2021 and £270M in 2022, with an 8% growth. Total growth over the period was 14% in 2020, 16% in 2021, and 20% in 2022, averaging 18%. The Trends Overview table indicates that in Q1 2020, total DAU was 3.2M and total MAU was 12.5M, with no change data available.

page.content: In 2021, DAU increased to 3.4M and MAU to 13.0M, reflecting a 6.25% growth. By 2022, DAU reached 3.8M, and MAU grew to 14.0M, showing an 11.76% increase. For Q2 2020, DAU was 3.5M and MAU was 12.8M, with no change data available.
